videos.title: Lecture 02: Fundamental Methods of Projection Theory

video_metadata.video_tags: Projection theory, Fourier analysis, Kaufman, Falconer, Vinh, finite field
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 18.156 Projection Theory, Spring 2025
Instructor: Lawrence D Guth
View the complete course: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-156-projection-theory-spring-2025
YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP62K9_7Pg26ytrAOXwXFpN43

We introduce two fundamental methods to study projections, developed by Kaufman and Falconer in the 1960s and 70s. The first is based on double counting. The second is based on Fourier analysis.

More information at https://ocw.mit.edu/terms
More courses at https://ocw.mit.edu

videos.title: Lecture 07: Applications of the Large Sieve to Number Theory

video_metadata.video_tags: Large sieve, projection theory, Bombieri-Vinogradov
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 18.156 Projection Theory, Spring 2025

YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP62K9_7Pg26ytrAOXwXFpN43

We discuss one of the main applications of the large sieve in number theory: the Bombieri-Vinogradov theorem about the distribution of prime numbers modulo q for different q.

videos.title: Lecture 08: The Szemeredi-Trotter Theorem

video_metadata.video_tags: Incidence geometry, projection theory, Szemeredi-Trotter
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 18.156 Projection Theory, Spring 2025
Instructor: Lawrence D Guth
View the complete course: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-156-projection-theory-spring-2025
YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP62K9_7Pg26ytrAOXwXFpN43

The Szemeredi-Trotter theorem gives the sharp answer to a natural discrete projection problem in the plane. The proof of the theorem is based on topology, and it is completely different from the proofs we have explored earlier.

License: Creative Commons BY-NC-SA
More information at https://ocw.mit.edu/terms
More courses at https://ocw.mit.edu
Support OCW at http://ow.ly/a1If50zVRlQ

videos.title: Lecture 09: Reflections on the Szemeredi-Trotter Theorem

video_metadata.video_tags: Szemeredi-Trotter, Furstenberg, Marstrand, projection theory, complex numbers
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 18.156 Projection Theory, Spring 2025
Instructor: Lawrence D Guth

YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP62K9_7Pg26ytrAOXwXFpN43

We discuss the analogy between the Szemeredi-Trotter theorem and the exceptional set problem. We explain why it is hard to adapt the proof of Szemeredi-Trotter to the exceptional set problem.
